articles.headline: No. 17 St. John's survives Providence push to win 11th straight, 79-69 over Friars
articles.description: — Dylan Darling had 23 points and eight rebounds and No. 17 St. John's scored eight straight points after a fracas that resulted in six ejections to take the lead and earn its 11th straight victory, a 79-69 win over Providence on Saturday.

articles.headline: No. 5 Iowa State snaps No. 9 Kansas' winning streak, 74-56
articles.description: — Milan Momcilovic scored 18 points and No. 5 Iowa State shook off a slow start to defeat No. 9 Kansas 74-56 on Saturday, snapping the Jayhawks’ eight-game winning streak.

articles.headline: Pennick and Navy win 84-80 in OT against Colgate
articles.description: — Jordan Pennick scored 22 points and Austin Benigni added five points in overtime as Navy took down Colgate 84-80 on Saturday.

article.headline: Seth Trimble hits late 3 to lift No. 14 UNC past No. 4 Duke 71-68 in stunning rivalry finish
article.description: — Seth Trimble made a corner 3-pointer with 0.4 seconds left to lift No. 14 North Carolina past No. 4 Duke 71-68 in a stunning finish Saturday night, which included fans storming the court prematurely before having to clear out for the Blue Devils to...
article.linkText: Seth Trimble hits late 3 to lift No. 14 UNC past No. 4 Duke 71-68 in stunning rivalry finish

images.name: Fans storm court twice after Seth Trimble's heroic 3 for UNC vs. Duke
images.caption: After Seth Trimble drains a go-ahead triple for UNC in final second, the fans storm the court but have to quickly get off the court before the Tar Heels can put away Duke.

tracking.trackingName: NCB_One-Play (Fans storm court twice after Seth Trimble's heroic 3 for UNC vs. Duke) 2026/02/07 ESHEET

categories.description: Duke Blue Devils @ North Carolina Tar Heels

event.description: Duke Blue Devils @ North Carolina Tar Heels

keywords: Duke Blue Devils @ North Carolina Tar Heels
article.story: CHAPEL HILL, N.C. -- — Seth Trimble made a corner 3-pointer with 0.4 seconds left to lift No. 14 North Carolina past No. 4 Duke 71-68 in a stunning finish Saturday night, which included fans storming the court prematurely before having to clear out for the Blue Devils to get one desperate final play. Trimble's shot on the catch off a feed from Derek Dixon swished cleanly through the net with apparently no time left, sending the Tar Heels rushing onto the court to celebrate followed closely by the fans at a frantic, ear-ringing roar. But a review determined there was still 0.4 seconds left, prompting the Smith Center's public-address announcer to repeatedly tell fans to clear the court. Once they did, Duke had a final chance, but Isaiah Evans couldn't get a clean handle on the long inbound pass to near halfcourt to get off a tying shot to end it — prompting another court-storming that stuck this time. It capped a night in which UNC trailed by 13 in the second half and never led until Trimble's final shot. ------ Get poll alerts and updates on the AP Top 25 throughout the season. Sign up here. AP college basketball: https://apnews.com/hub/ap-top-25-college-basketball-poll and https://apnews.com/hub/college-basketball
